original_image_tags: A scan of the reverse side of an aged, light green/tan sheet of paper. The paper is discolored with age and has three punch holes along the top edge. The content visible is from "bleed-through" of the front side and is reversed. There are several stamped marks, also in reverse, and some handwritten annotations in the upper left corner.

The overall appearance is that of the back of a document, likely a routing or memorandum sheet, showing the ghost of the typed front content and several official received stamps, primarily from the FBI and the Air Force.

elements.description: Back of a typed document with multiple inverted ink stamps and handwritten annotations. Visible stamps include 'S DEPT OF JUSTICE', 'RECEIVED', and a boxed 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION BY SPECIAL MESSENGER MAY 11 1945'. Faint reversed typed text is visible through the paper.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the image shows the back of a document with reversed bleed-through text, multiple stamps, and handwritten annotations; Description B adds specific quoted stamp text ('S DEPT OF JUSTICE', 'RECEIVED', and a date) that Description A omits but does not contradict.
